creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Li-Wen Hsu from comment #1)

I'll try. 

First, wording … 


Something like a stub in a Japanese page in 1999, 
<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-doc/blob/af10ba312066057ebb85f0dc9cd1e2b0a688d76e/ja_JP.eucJP/man/man8/loader.8#L329>


<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/commit/aa203600d542b9528146c176e8cae1044b48a98f#diff-939d8a2cd5a763b38b4f5aeeb24915345ae2555f0a263420601b2c981bd3fd95R157-R160> (1998-12-15): 

> set boot_verbose
> 
> Setting this variable causes extra debugging information to be printed
> by the kernel during the boot phase.


<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/commit/9efec336c596e55d05642bbbfc322493c0db6dbf#diff-f0a9397a2836fdd3eb9a9818f012e799bf7b550d91b5d74b9a9079d61eba5443R61-R68> (2002-07-16) NB _any_: 

> 			# The following boot_ variables are enabled
>			# by setting them to any value.

…

> #boot_verbose=""	# Causes extra debugging information to be printed


<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/commit/9873c807dac1f8b83b9f7bc9a8d4e318ea295b55#diff-bee0bbebe8ee2e1ae33ee3d286a550ca35eb6b4931c686eaedc7b0c100a71839R371-R373> (2021-07-14): 

> .It Va boot_verbose
> Setting this variable causes extra debugging information to be printed
> by the kernel during the boot phase.

– this page is online for FreeBSD 14.0-CURRENT at: 

<https://www.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=loader_simp&sektion=8&manpath=FreeBSD+14.0-current>
